content_en:
Qadi Iyad's methodology in Ikmal al-Mu'lim builds on and transforms the foundation laid by al-Maziri in al-Mu'lim. Al-Maziri's commentary, while important, was a partial work that addressed selected hadiths in Sahih Muslim rather than covering it comprehensively. Qadi Iyad's task was to complete the commentary on the portions al-Maziri had not addressed, to expand and revise al-Maziri's existing discussions where he found them insufficient, and to integrate both into a unified, comprehensive commentary on the full text of Sahih Muslim.

Qadi Iyad's own contributions to the commentary are characterized by an extraordinary command of the Arabic language, a deep familiarity with the Maliki legal tradition and its Andalusian variant, and a broad engagement with the full range of Islamic sciences including theology, hadith criticism, and historical scholarship. His linguistic discussions of difficult or rare terms in the hadiths of Sahih Muslim are among the finest available and have been praised by scholars of Arabic across the centuries. His legal discussions present the Maliki positions with the clarity and authority of a scholar who was not only trained in the tradition but had applied it in judicial practice.

The theological dimension of Ikmal al-Mu'lim reflects Qadi Iyad's engagement with the Ash'ari theological tradition that dominated Maliki scholarship in his era and region. His discussions of the divine attributes, the nature of prophecy, and the eschatological events described in Sahih Muslim's traditions follow the Ash'ari approach with sophistication and care. These discussions have been important resources for subsequent Ash'ari theologians engaging with the hadith evidence for their positions.

The relationship between Ikmal al-Mu'lim and an-Nawawi's later Al-Minhaj is important. An-Nawawi, who wrote the commentary that would eventually become the standard scholarly reference on Sahih Muslim, drew heavily on Ikmal al-Mu'lim, citing Qadi Iyad frequently and building on his discussions in many places. Through Al-Minhaj, Qadi Iyad's scholarship has exercised an indirect but significant influence on all subsequent engagement with Sahih Muslim.
content_id:
Metodologi Qadhi Iyadh dalam Ikmal al-Mu'lim membangun di atas dan mentransformasikan fondasi yang diletakkan al-Maziri dalam Al-Mu'lim. Komentar al-Maziri, meskipun penting, adalah karya parsial yang menangani pilihan-pilihan tradisi dari Shahih Muslim tetapi meninggalkannya tidak lengkap pada saat kematiannya. Qadhi Iyadh menyelesaikan proyek, menangani tradisi-tradisi yang al-Maziri tinggalkan dan memperluas diskusi tentang yang al-Maziri tangani.

Pendekatan Qadhi Iyadh terhadap sanad lebih sistematis dari al-Maziri, mencerminkan pelatihan hadis yang lebih mendalam dari ulama yang menulis setelah kodifikasi penuh ilmu rijal oleh Ibn Abi Hatim, al-Bukhari, dan yang lainnya. Untuk setiap hadis yang signifikan, Qadhi Iyadh memberikan penilaian tentang sanad, mengidentifikasi perawi-perawi kunci dan posisi kritikus tentang keandalan mereka, dan membahas pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang di mana transmisi Muslim berbeda dari al-Bukhari atau sumber-sumber lain.

Dimensi linguistik dari Ikmal al-Mu'lim sangat dikembangkan. Qadhi Iyadh memberikan penjelasan terperinci tentang kosakata dan gramatikal yang sulit dari teks-teks Shahih Muslim, mengacu pada kamus-kamus bahasa Arab awal dan sastra untuk mendukung interpretasinya. Penjelasan-penjelasan linguistik ini sering memperjelas tidak hanya arti harfiah sebuah teks tetapi nuansa-nuansa yang memiliki konsekuensi hukum, membuat Ikmal al-Mu'lim menjadi sumber yang tak ternilai bagi siapa pun yang mengerjakan teks Shahih Muslim yang menghadirkan kesulitan linguistik.
content_ar:
Qadi Iyad's methodology in Ikmal al-Mu'lim builds on and transforms the foundation laid by al-Maziri in al-Mu'lim. Al-Maziri's commentary, while important, was a partial work that addressed selected hadiths in Sahih Muslim rather than covering it comprehensively. Qadi Iyad's task was to complete the commentary on the portions al-Maziri had not addressed, to expand and revise al-Maziri's existing discussions where he found them insufficient, and to integrate both into a unified, comprehensive commentary on the full text of Sahih Muslim.

Qadi Iyad's own contributions to the commentary are characterized by an extraordinary command of the Arabic language, a deep familiarity with the Maliki legal tradition and its Andalusian variant, and a broad engagement with the full range of Islamic sciences including theology, الحديث criticism, and historical scholarship. His linguistic discussions of difficult or rare terms in the hadiths of Sahih Muslim are among the finest available and have been praised by scholars of Arabic across the centuries. His legal discussions present the Maliki positions with the clarity and authority of a scholar who was not only trained in the tradition but had applied it in judicial practice.

The theological dimension of Ikmal al-Mu'lim reflects Qadi Iyad's engagement with the Ash'ari theological tradition that dominated Maliki scholarship in his era and region. His discussions of the divine attributes, the nature of prophecy, and the eschatological events described in Sahih Muslim's traditions follow the Ash'ari approach with sophistication and care. These discussions have been important resources for subsequent Ash'ari theologians engaging with the الحديث evidence for their positions.

The relationship between Ikmal al-Mu'lim and an-Nawawi's later Al-Minhaj is important. An-Nawawi, who wrote the commentary that would eventually become the standard scholarly reference on Sahih Muslim, drew heavily on Ikmal al-Mu'lim, citing Qadi Iyad frequently and building on his discussions in many places. Through Al-Minhaj, Qadi Iyad's scholarship has exercised an indirect but significant influence on all subsequent engagement with Sahih Muslim.
